scope: custom: core_engine + combat + derived_stats -- R3 cross-attribute parity sweep (armature reset 2026-05-29)
note: The headline validation: does the corrected model achieve cross-attribute parity at EQUAL budget (the correctly-framed C-04 = Ω-d no-dominant-strategy)? Wires R1 (resolution + Agi sigma-leverage) + R2 (consequence + Str/End) into one decisive 6-10s exchange. HONEST RESULT: C-04 CLOSED (Agi no longer dominates, now weakest at 40.1%) but dominance MOVED to Endurance wound-gate depth (End-heavy 63.4%, CI excludes band) -- reproduces PP-717's independent End-dominance finding. Parity does NOT yet pass; the End/Str/Agi channel magnitudes are the Class-C tuning knob (Jordan calibration; project-owner contract bars Claude from retconning balance). Class A = canonical character-budget (31-pt buy, attr 1-7, creation max 5); reuses R1/R2/M3 (already ledgered). Class M = sweep-method params (trial count, Wilson z, acceptance band, exchange cap -- statistical harness, NOT game mechanics). PROPOSAL -- no canon edited; the sweep is the tuning oracle.

entries.quoted_text: Point pool at creation: 31 points across 10 attributes. Minimum 1 per attribute.
Creation max: 5 (one attribute only; all others <= 4). Advancement max: 7.
trials per matchup -- statistical sample size at the M8/M8b harness scale (N=2000-4000/matchup); Wilson CI shrinks with N.
95% confidence z-score (1.96) for the Wilson score interval on a binomial proportion -- standard statistic used by the M8/M8b sweeps.
archetype matchups 40-60% (skill §8.8 band), no inversion, no non-viable baseline build.
